data.description:
## Context

Placeholder epic to brainstorm G-1 from the DSO gap analysis (~/dso_rebar_plan_review_gap_report.md, §3). rebar's plan-review evaluates each finding independently through Pass 2/3, so it can emit advisory findings that pull the author in opposite directions (e.g. G5 'split this unit' vs G3 'children under-cover parent, add work here'; T4 'add a compat path' vs A1/T4 'gratuitous compat'). Because the protocol is remediate-and-re-run, contradictory coaching can waste review cycles and induce oscillation (fix A -> re-trigger B -> forever).

DSO's answer: a review-protocol Stage-2 that groups findings by target, classifies opposing-direction pairs with a typed taxonomy (add_vs_remove / more_vs_less / strict_vs_flexible / expand_vs_reduce), and resolves via a ladder (critical beats minor; two blocking-tier conflicts escalate to a human; minor never conflicts).

## Open design question (why this is its own epic)

The report proposes a NEW single-turn LLM conflict pass between Pass 3 and Pass 4. That cuts against rebar's grain (minimise LLM calls; keep decisions deterministic/arithmetic). A lower-level alternative: detect opposing pairs DETERMINISTICALLY over the Pass-4 coach's already-chosen (move, subject) tuples -- two findings naming the same subject with opposing moves are a detectable conflict with no extra LLM call. Which level is right, how to render the conflict in the verdict, and how it interacts with the block/advisory posture + the rising-floor re-review are the design questions to resolve in a dedicated brainstorming session.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] A decision on the detection level (deterministic-over-coach-tuples vs new LLM pass vs hybrid)
- [ ] A defined conflict taxonomy + resolution ladder mapped onto rebar's block/advisory posture
- [ ] Interaction with rising-floor re-review + oscillation signal (G-2) worked out
- [ ] Evidence (sidecar) that contradictory coaching actually occurs before building

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] This epic holds the design rationale and links to the G-1 section of the gap report
- [ ] A brainstorming session produces a concrete implementation plan (child stories) before any code is written
- [ ] Placeholder only: NOT to be claimed/implemented until the design session concludes
data.title: Plan-review: cross-finding conflict detection (G-1)
